# GCM Repository Review TODO

<!-- Generated By: Cursor (claude-4-sonnet) -->

This document contains coarse-grained review tasks for the GCM (Git Checkout Master) repository. Each section represents a major area that needs human review and validation.

## 🔄 GitHub Workflows

### Review Tasks
- [x] **PR Check Workflow** - Validate that `pr-check.yml` correctly runs lint and unit tests
- [x] **Coverage Check Workflow** - Verify `coverage-check.yml` properly enforces threshold and posts PR comments
- [x] **Coverage Badge Workflow** - Confirm `coverage-badge.yml` updates badge correctly on main branch pushes
- [x] **Workflow Integration** - Ensure all workflows work together and don't conflict
- [x] **Environment Setup** - Validate that workflows use correct Python version and dependency installation

### Key Files
- `.github/workflows/pr-check.yml`
- `.github/workflows/coverage-check.yml` 
- `.github/workflows/coverage-badge.yml`

---

## 📋 Requirements Documentation

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **Requirements Completeness** - Verify `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md` covers all functional requirements
- [ ] **Edge Case Coverage** - Validate that edge cases and error scenarios are comprehensive
- [ ] **Configuration Schema** - Review YAML configuration structure and validate against implementation
- [ ] **Non-Functional Requirements** - Check performance, reliability, usability, and portability requirements
- [ ] **Scope Boundaries** - Confirm VPN management is properly excluded from scope
- [ ] **Dependency Requirements** - Validate Python 3.8+ and Git 2.0+ requirements

### Key Files
- `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md`

---

## 🧪 Unit Test Plan

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **Test Coverage Mapping** - Verify `docs/UNIT_TEST_PLAN.md` maps 1:1 to actual test implementations
- [ ] **Configuration Tests** - Review CONFIG-01 through CONFIG-10 test coverage
- [ ] **Git Operations Tests** - Validate GIT-01 through GIT-17 test scenarios
- [ ] **Main Workflow Tests** - Check MAIN-01 through MAIN-16 test completeness
- [ ] **Test Validation** - Manually validate that test descriptions match implementation intent
- [ ] **Missing Test Cases** - Identify any requirements not covered by unit tests

### Key Files
- `docs/UNIT_TEST_PLAN.md`
- `tests/unit/test_config.py`
- `tests/unit/test_git_repository.py`
- `tests/unit/test_gcm_main.py`

---

## 🔗 Integration Test Plan

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **End-to-End Coverage** - Review `docs/INTEGRATION_TEST_PLAN.md` for comprehensive workflow testing
- [ ] **Repository State Tests** - Validate REPO-01 through REPO-06 test scenarios
- [ ] **Branch Management Tests** - Check BRANCH-01 through BRANCH-05 test coverage
- [ ] **Remote Configuration Tests** - Review REMOTE-01 through REMOTE-06 scenarios
- [ ] **Conflict Scenarios** - Validate CONFLICT-01 through CONFLICT-05 error handling
- [ ] **Provider Detection Tests** - Check PROVIDER-01 through PROVIDER-04 test cases
- [ ] **Test Implementation** - Verify integration tests actually implement the planned scenarios

### Key Files
- `docs/INTEGRATION_TEST_PLAN.md`
- `tests/integration/test_repository_states.py`
- `tests/integration/conftest.py`
- `tests/helpers/git_repo_factory.py`

## 🔨 Make System

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **Makefile Structure** - Review modular makefile organization and dependencies
- [ ] **Environment Setup** - Validate `make/env.mk` virtual environment and uv package manager setup
- [ ] **Test Targets** - Check `make/test.mk` test execution and coverage generation
- [ ] **Lint Targets** - Review `make/lint.mk` code quality and formatting
- [ ] **Dependency Management** - Verify `make requirements-dev` installs all necessary packages
- [ ] **Cross-Platform Compatibility** - Ensure make targets work on Linux, macOS, and Windows

### Key Files
- `Makefile`
- `make/env.mk`
- `make/test.mk`
- `make/lint.mk`

---

## ⚙️ Configuration Structure

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **Configuration Schema** - Review YAML configuration structure and validate against requirements
- [ ] **Provider Configuration** - Check GitHub and GitLab provider settings
- [ ] **Behavior Configuration** - Validate destructive operation confirmations and parallel operations
- [ ] **Configuration Discovery** - Review local vs global configuration precedence
- [ ] **Default Values** - Verify default configuration values are appropriate
- [ ] **Configuration Validation** - Check error handling for invalid configuration files

### Key Files
- `gcm.py` (GCMConfig class)
- Configuration examples in requirements documentation

---

## 📦 Python Requirements

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **Runtime Dependencies** - Review `requirements.txt` for minimal necessary dependencies
- [ ] **Development Dependencies** - Check `requirements-dev.txt` for testing and development tools
- [ ] **Version Constraints** - Validate version requirements are appropriate and not overly restrictive
- [ ] **Dependency Security** - Review dependencies for known security issues
- [ ] **Optional Dependencies** - Check if any dependencies could be made optional
- [ ] **Standard Library Usage** - Verify maximum use of Python standard library

### Key Files
- `requirements.txt`
- `requirements-dev.txt`

---

## 💻 Source Implementation

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **Code Architecture** - Review overall code structure and organization
- [ ] **Error Handling** - Validate comprehensive error handling and user-friendly error messages
- [ ] **Logging** - Check logging implementation for debugging and user feedback
- [ ] **Command Line Interface** - Review argument parsing and help documentation
- [ ] **Git Operations** - Validate git command execution and output parsing
- [ ] **Configuration Management** - Check configuration loading and merging logic
- [ ] **Provider Detection** - Review git hosting provider detection logic
- [ ] **Branch Management** - Validate trunk branch detection and branch cleanup logic
- [ ] **Fork Management** - Check fork detection and synchronization logic
- [ ] **Parallel Operations** - Review concurrent operation implementation

### Key Files
- `gcm.py` (main implementation)

## 🧪 Test Implementation

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **Unit Test Quality** - Review unit test implementation quality and coverage
- [ ] **Integration Test Quality** - Check integration test implementation and real-world scenarios
- [ ] **Test Helpers** - Validate test helper classes and utilities
- [ ] **Mock Usage** - Review appropriate use of mocks vs real operations
- [ ] **Test Data** - Check test data generation and repository factory
- [ ] **Test Isolation** - Validate tests don't interfere with each other
- [ ] **Test Documentation** - Review test documentation and comments
- [ ] **Test Execution** - Verify all tests can be run successfully
- [ ] **Coverage Reporting** - Check coverage reports are accurate and useful

### Key Files
- `tests/unit/` (all unit test files)
- `tests/integration/` (all integration test files)
- `tests/helpers/` (test helper utilities)

## 📊 Overall Assessment

### Review Tasks
- [ ] **Requirements Traceability** - Verify all requirements are implemented and tested
- [ ] **Code Quality** - Review overall code quality, readability, and maintainability
- [ ] **Documentation Completeness** - Check that all code is properly documented
- [ ] **Security Review** - Validate security considerations in configuration and file handling
- [ ] **Performance Review** - Check performance characteristics and optimization opportunities
- [ ] **Usability Assessment** - Review user experience and error handling
- [ ] **Deployment Readiness** - Validate the project is ready for production use

## ✅ Validation Checklist

After completing each review section, mark the validation status:

- [ ] **GitHub Workflows** - Validated and working correctly
- [ ] **Requirements** - Complete and accurate
- [ ] **Unit Test Plan** - Comprehensive and well-implemented
- [ ] **Integration Test Plan** - Covers all scenarios and implemented
- [ ] **Make System** - Functional and cross-platform compatible
- [ ] **Configuration Structure** - Well-designed and documented
- [ ] **Python Requirements** - Minimal and secure
- [ ] **Source Implementation** - High quality and maintainable
- [ ] **Test Implementation** - Comprehensive and reliable
